staff is presumed dead unless there are grounds for presuming that the 
death was not due to an accident.

6.3.9 No reimbursement of expenses, indemnity or allowance under Article 6.3.7 shall

occupational disease arose from a situation in which they intentionally caused or 
contributed to the death or injury of another person.  
6.3.10 (a) Members of staff who suffer an alleged accident at work or those entitled 
under them shall report it to the Directorate General Human Resources. 
Where the alleged accident at work results in death or where it is impossible 
for the member of staff or those entitled under them to report it, this may be 
done by any member of their family or any other person with knowledge of 
the facts.

34. (a) From the date of appointment, the ECB shall provide members of staff with full insurance 
cover against all reasonable and customary medical and dental expenses incurred as a 
result of an occupational disease or accident at work in the manner provided for in the Staff 
Rules. This insurance shall be provided free of charge to members of staff. Members of 
staff may claim solely against the insurer on the basis of the ECB insurance policy.

demos.question: What indemnity is paid if a staff member dies as a result of an accident at work?
demos.reasoning: The context provided includes information on the indemnity provided by the European Central Bank (ECB) in the event of a staff member's death due to an accident at work. According to the ECB Conditions of Employment, an indemnity equal to five times the deceased member of staff’s annual basic salary is payable. This indemnity is directed to the persons known to the ECB and designated by the deceased member of staff. If no such persons are designated, the indemnity is paid to their spouse and children, in accordance with the law of succession governing the deceased member of staff’s estate.
demos.answer: If a staff member dies as a result of an accident at work, the indemnity paid is equal to five times the deceased member of staff’s annual basic salary. This indemnity is paid to the persons designated by the deceased or, if none, to their spouse and children according to the applicable law of succession. (ECB Conditions of Employment, Page 16)

demos.question: What installation allowance am I entitled to when I relocate to take up my appointment at the ECB?
demos.answer: Subject to the conditions laid down in the Staff Rules, the ECB grants an installation allowance equal to 200% of the basic monthly salary for members of staff entitled to a household allowance, or 100% of the basic monthly salary in other cases. (ECB Conditions of Employment, page 12)

demos.question: If I want to apply internally for another position at the ECB, am I eligible if I haven't yet completed my probationary period, and how long does my probationary period normally last?
demos.answer: Eligibility for internal selection procedures is reserved for members of staff with an indefinite or fixed-term contract, and graduate programme participants, who have successfully completed their probationary period at the closing date for applications. Appointments are subject to a probationary period of six months unless the Chief Services Officer, on behalf of the Executive Board, decides to waive it, although in exceptional circumstances a longer period may be determined. (ECB Staff Rules, pages 33, 47)

demos.question: What is the duration of the probationary period for new appointments?
demos.answer: Appointments are subject to a probationary period of six months unless the Chief Services Officer, on behalf of the Executive Board, decides to waive it. In exceptional circumstances, the Chief Services Officer may determine a probationary period longer than six months, which may in any case be extended up to a maximum of twelve months. (ECB Staff Rules, page 47)
